verse: Unjust anger can never be justified, for anger drives a person to his ruin. (Sir 1:22)
A fool offers full vent to anger. but a wise man quietly holds it back. (Pro 29:11)
A short-tempered man provokes conflicts. but he who is slow to anger quiets contention. (Pro 15:18)
Do not be quickly moved to anger. For anger resides in the bosom of the foolish. (Eccl 7:9)
Let every man be quick to listen, but slow to speak and slow to anger; for your anger does not produce God’s righteousness. (Jas 1:19-20)
The fool immediately reveals his anger. But whoever ignores insults is prudent. (Pro 12:16)
Whoever is slow to anger is governed by much prudence. But whoever has a hasty temper exalts his foolishness. (Pro 14:29)
One who is slow to anger is better than a strong one. And whoever controls his temper is better than one who captures cities. (Pro 16:32)
A person with hot temper will pay the penalty. For if you deliver him, you will only have to do it again. (Pro 19:19)
Do not become like the horse and the mule, which have no understanding. Their temper must be constrained with bit and bridle, or it will not stay near you. (Ps 32:9)

For three wicked deeds of Edom, and for four, I won’t hold back the punishment, in so far as he has been pursuing his brother with the sword and has outraged his brother’s compassion, and he has kept his anger perpetually and has held onto his indignation until the end. (Amos 1:11)
Be angry, but do not be willing to sin. Do not let the sun set over your anger. Do not give room for the devil. (Eph 4:26-27)

Refrain from strife, and you will diminish your sins. For an ill-tempered man enflames conflict, and a sinful man troubles his friends, and he casts hostility into the midst of those who have peace. (Sir 28:8-9)

A fire will burn, in proportion to the fuel, and strife will increase, in proportion to the obstinacy. The greater is the power of a man, the greater will be his anger; the greater his wealth, the greater his wrath will be. (Sir 28:10)
Do not avenge yourselves, dearest ones; Instead, leave room for God’s wrath. For it is written: “Vengeance is mine. I shall give retribution, says the Lord.” (Rom 12:19)
See to it that no one repays evil for evil to anyone. Instead, always pursue whatever is good, with one another and with all. (1 Thes 5:15)
Do not seek revenge, neither should you hold any grudge against your own people. You shall love your friend as yourself. I am the Lord. (Lev 19:18)
Do not say, “I will repay evil.” Wait for the Lord, and he will save you. (Pro 20:22)
Does anyone harbor anger against another and expect healing from the Lord? (Sir 28:3)
The quick-tempered will act foolishly. (Pro 14:17)
Let all bitterness and anger and indignation and outcry and slander be taken away from you, along with all malice. (Eph 4:31)
Cease from anger and forsake wrath; Do not fret—it leads only to evil. (Ps 37:8)
An angry man stirs up strife. And whoever is easily angered sins a lot. (Pro 29:22)

You have heard that it was said to your ancestors: ‘You shall not murder; whoever will have murdered shall be liable to judgment.’ But I say to you, that anyone who becomes angry with his brother or sister shall be liable to judgment. (Matt 5:21-22)
Anger and fury are both abominable, yet a sinner holds on to them. (Sir 27:30)
It is honorable to refrain from strife, but every fool rushes to quarrel. (Pro 20:3)
We who are strong have an obligation to bear with the failings of the weak, and not to please ourselves. Let each of us please our neighbor for his good, to build him up. (Rom 15:1-2)
A mild answer turns away wrath. But a harsh word stirs up anger. (Pro 15:1)
You must put away all these things: anger, wrath, malice, slander, and abusive language from your mouth. (Col 3:8)
Do not be willing to be a friend to an angry man, and do not associate with a furious man. lest you learn his ways and entangle yourself in a snare. (Pro 22:24-25)
You shall not hate your brother in your heart, but reprove him, lest you incur sin because of him. (Lev 19:17)
Sensible people are slow to anger; and it is their glory to overlook an offense. (Pro 19:11)
Stay away from stupid and senseless controversies; you know that they breed quarrels. (2 Tim 2:23)
When evil was spoken against him, he did not speak evil. When he suffered, he did not threaten. But he handed himself over to him who judges justly. (1 Pet 2:23)
The Lord said to Cain: “Why are you angry, and why is your face fallen? (Gen 4:6)
Pride was not created for human beings, nor was an angry temperament created for the those born of women. (Sir 10:18)
Those who are kind benefit themselves, but the cruel do themselves harm. (Pro 11:17)
Be merciful, just as your Father is also merciful. (Luk 6:36)
Do not get angry with your neighbor for every wrong, and do not resort to acts of violence. (Sir 10:6)
The Lord is compassionate and merciful, slow to anger and full of steadfast love. (Ps 145:8)
You are God’s chosen people, holy and beloved. So then, you must clothe yourselves with compassion, kindness, humility, meekness, and patience. (Col 3:12)
Remember the commandments, and do not be angry with your neighbor; Remember the covenant of the Most High and overlook the ignorant offenses of your neighbor. (Sir 28:7)

As a prisoner in the Lord, I beg you to walk in a manner worthy of the vocation to which you have been called: with all humility and gentleness, with patience, bearing one another in love. (Eph 4:1-2)

Finally, be you all of one mind, compassionate, love for one another, merciful, meek, and humble. Do not repay evil for evil or cursing with cursing; instead, give back a blessing, because to this you were called, that you might inherit a blessing. (1 Pet 3:8-9)
The beginning of strife is like letting out water; so, quit before the quarrel breaks out. (Pro 17:14)
His anger is but for a moment, and his favor is for a lifetime. (Ps 30:5)
The servant of the Lord must not be quarrelsome, but instead he must be kind toward everyone, able to teach, patient, admonishing them that resist the truth. (2 Tim 2:24-25)
Let your gentleness be known to everyone. The Lord is near. (Phil 4:5)
Be always humble, gentle, and patient. Show your love by being tolerant with one another. Make every effort to preserve the unity of the Spirit in the bond of peace. (Eph 4:2-3)

If we say that we are in the light, while hating a brother or sister, we are still in the darkness to this very hour. If we love others, we live in the light, and so there is nothing in us that will cause us to stumble. (1 Jn 2:9-10)
